skill.name: gsap
skill.description: GSAP reference for building HyperFrames animations with tweens, timelines, easing, staggered motion, responsive matchMedia patterns, and audio visualizer helpers.
skill.summary: Create performant GSAP animations for HyperFrames compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical combinations, but review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, credential access, or exfiltration. The meaningful risks are a legitimate helper script that invokes ffmpeg on user-supplied media and writes a caller-selected JSON output file. Most command, crypto, SAM, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown examples and audio-analysis terminology.

medium_findings.title: External ffmpeg Invocation in Helper Script
medium_findings.description: The audio extraction helper builds an ffmpeg command from a user-supplied media path and executes it with subprocess.run. The command uses an argument list instead of shell=True, which reduces injection risk, but it still runs an external media parser on untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is real and semantically tied to ffmpeg decoding. Command injection is not evident because arguments are passed as a list without shell execution.

medium_findings.title: Caller-Controlled Output File Write
medium_findings.description: The helper writes generated audio data to the path provided by the --output argument. This is expected utility behavior, but misuse can overwrite files writable by the current user.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write path is directly configurable through argparse and passed to open(). No evidence of hidden persistence or unauthorized file reads was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Browser Network Loads
low_findings.description: The reference document includes CDN script tags for GSAP and a synchronous XMLHttpRequest example for local audio-data.json. These are user-facing examples, not hidden exfiltration behavior, and the fetch mention explicitly warns against asynchronous loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network-related strings are present, but they appear in examples for loading public GSAP assets and a local JSON file. No credential or data exfiltration endpoint is shown.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code, JavaScript snippets, GSAP method names, or animation values. They are not executable shell backticks and do not create command execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is prose or fenced JavaScript/HTML/CSS examples. No Ruby interpreter, shell execution, or dynamic command construction is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Data and Weak Crypto Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance alerts map to audio constants, FFT operations, min/max math, and media frequency wording. No credential store access, hashing workflow, reconnaissance command, or cryptographic decision was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are substrings or ordinary numeric/audio operations. The files do not reference Windows credential paths, crypto APIs, or host enumeration commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: External ffmpeg Invocation in Helper Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The audio extraction helper builds an ffmpeg command from a user-supplied media path and executes it with subprocess.run. The command uses an argument list instead of shell=True, which reduces injection risk, but it still runs an external media parser on untrusted input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is real and semantically tied to ffmpeg decoding. Command injection is not evident because arguments are passed as a list without shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Caller-Controlled Output File Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper writes generated audio data to the path provided by the --output argument. This is expected utility behavior, but misuse can overwrite files writable by the current user.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write path is directly configurable through argparse and passed to open(). No evidence of hidden persistence or unauthorized file reads was found.

content.value_statement: HyperFrames animation work needs precise timing, cleanup, and performance choices. This skill gives GSAP patterns for tweens, timelines, typewriter effects, and audio-reactive visuals.
actual_capabilities: Explains GSAP tween methods including to, from, fromTo, and set.
Documents timeline sequencing with labels, nesting, defaults, and position parameters.
Provides performance guidance for transform-based animation and quickTo updates.
Describes responsive and reduced-motion patterns using gsap.matchMedia.
Includes typewriter effect patterns with cursor timing and backspacing behavior.
Includes an audio data extraction helper for visualizer timelines.
limitations: It is a reference skill, not a complete animation framework.
The audio helper requires local Python, numpy, and ffmpeg.
It does not validate visual quality or render animations automatically.
Examples assume HyperFrames composition rules and deterministic timeline construction.

use_cases.title: Create Product Motion
use_cases.description: Designers can build polished entrance, emphasis, and exit animations with clear timing and easing guidance.

use_cases.title: Implement Deterministic Scenes
use_cases.description: Frontend engineers can structure GSAP timelines that are predictable for HyperFrames capture workflows.

use_cases.title: Build Audio-Reactive Visuals
use_cases.description: Video creators can pre-extract audio bands and drive canvas or DOM visuals from timeline callbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Basic Entrance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GSAP skill to create a simple HyperFrames entrance animation for these elements. Prefer transform and opacity properties.

prompt_templates.title: Sequence a Timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use timeline labels and position parameters to sequence this scene. Explain the timing choices and cleanup needs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Typewriter Motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a deterministic typewriter effect with cursor states, pauses, and backspacing. Keep it compatible with HyperFrames capture.

prompt_templates.title: Design Audio Visualizer
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan an audio-reactive GSAP visualizer using pre-extracted RMS and frequency bands. Include rendering approach, smoothing, and timeline construction.

output_examples.input: Request: Animate three headline lines into a product reveal.
output_examples.output: A timeline plan with staggered entrance timing, transform-based movement, opacity fades, labels, and playback cleanup notes.

output_examples.input: Request: Make a rotating typewriter word effect.
output_examples.output: A cursor state sequence that types each word, holds, backspaces, and hands off cleanly between text states.

output_examples.input: Request: Create an audio visualizer for a music clip.
output_examples.output: A workflow that extracts frame data, maps bass and treble bands to visual properties, and renders deterministic timeline updates.

best_practices: Prefer transform and opacity animation for smoother rendering.
Use timeline labels and position parameters instead of delay chains.
Kill or pause tweens when animated elements leave the scene.
anti_patterns: Do not animate layout properties when transforms can produce the same result.
Do not build HyperFrames timelines inside asynchronous fetch callbacks.
Do not combine svgOrigin and transformOrigin on the same SVG element.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps create GSAP animations for HyperFrames, including tweens, timelines, typewriter effects, and audio-reactive visuals.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: It includes reference examples and one Python helper script for extracting audio visualization data.

faq.question: Why does the security audit mention ffmpeg?
faq.answer: The audio helper uses ffmpeg to decode local audio or video files before creating visualization data.

faq.question: Can it load data from a network service?
faq.answer: No network service integration is provided. The documentation shows public CDN script tags and local audio-data loading.

faq.question: Is this suitable for accessibility-aware motion?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents gsap.matchMedia patterns, including reduced-motion handling.

faq.question: What should users install for audio extraction?
faq.answer: Users need Python 3.9 or later, numpy, and ffmpeg on the local machine.
